lessons.title: Psychiatric Injury Primary Victims
lessons.description: Let us begin with first principles. The law distinguishes between two categories of plaintiff in psychiatric injury cases: primary victims and secondary victims.

lessons.title: Psychiatric Injury Secondary Victims and the Kelly v Hennessy Test
lessons.description: A secondary victim is someone who suffers psychiatric injury not because they were in the zone of physical danger themselves, but because they witnessed — or learned of — the death or serious injury of another person.
